title: True or False - Boolean Logic
description: Learn about truth values and logical operations - the foundation of making decisions in code.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Let me teach you about booleans!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Named after George Boole! Booleans are values that are either True or False. They're how computers make decisions.

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: So everything is either true or false? Like yes or no?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Exactly! Binary logic. It's the foundation of all computer decision-making.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: We create booleans using comparisons. For example, you can ask 'is the wheat count greater than 5?' The answer is either True or False.

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: What comparison operators can I use?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: You can check if values are equal, not equal, less than, greater than, less or equal, and greater or equal. Each gives you a True or False answer!

objectives.description: Use the comparison operator, >. There are other comparison operators such as >, == and !=

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Perfect! Comparisons evaluate to True or False based on the condition.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: You can store boolean values in variables! Create a variable that stores the result of a comparison. It will hold either True or False!

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: So I can save the result of a comparison for later?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Exactly! This is useful when you need to check the same condition multiple times.

objectives.description: Create a boolean variable storing a comparison result starting with 'is_' to indicate it's a boolean
Use a comparison operator in the assignment

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Good variable names like 'is_ready' or 'has_wheat' make code self-documenting!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: You can combine booleans with logical operators like 'and', 'or', and 'not'. This lets you check multiple conditions at once!

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: So 'and' means both must be True?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Correct! 'or' means at least one must be True, and 'not' flips the result. Very powerful!

objectives.description: Create a complex boolean expression using and/or

postDialogues.name: Qubit
postDialogues.content: Logical operators let me express complex conditions!

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Exactly! Now you have the foundation for conditional statements. Let's learn how to make code execute based on these conditions!

rewards.description: Unlocked quest: Making Decisions - If Statements and Combining Conditions
